payload.answer: These memories conflict directly: one says Sam from Goldman has two children, the other says Sam from Goldman has no children. Because the system should preserve ambiguity and avoid auto-supersession, the safest action is keep_both until a later source resolves the discrepancy.
citations: Existing memory - Sam from Goldman has two children
New - Sam from Goldman has no children

entity_resolution.reason: The two statements appear to refer to the same person, but there is not enough backend context to resolve the identity beyond the shared name/affiliation.

payload.intent: Explain a direct contradiction between an existing memory and a new memory about Sam from Goldman’s children, while preserving ambiguity and not auto-overwriting either claim.

memory_cards.statement: Existing memory: Sam from Goldman has two children. New memory: Sam from Goldman has no children.

details: Detected a direct contradiction about the same entity.
No safe evidence was provided to determine which statement is correct.

repair_options: keep_both: retain both statements as conflicting and unresolved
reject_new: keep the existing memory and discard the new one
approve_supersession: accept the new statement only if later evidence confirms the old memory is wrong
edit: revise one or both statements only when a precise, supported correction is available
